events.title: ‘It’s a different world now’: Albanese calls snap national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis
events.description: Australian prime minister says US–Israeli war on Iran has shifted the international reality, likening current economic shock to Covid pandemic and Ukraine invasion Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese says the federal government is preparing new measures to help shield households from the economic fallout of the war in Iran, likening supply chain and fuel disruptions to the Covid-19 pandemic. Announcing a snap meeting of national cabinet on fuel supply set to take place on Thursday morning, the prime minister used a speech to the Australian Automotive Dealer Association in Sydney to warn the international reality had shifted because of the US and Israeli-led bombings. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It’s a different world now’: Albanese calls snap national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis

events.title: At least 23 people killed in suspected suicide attacks in north-eastern Nigeria
events.description: More than 100 others injured in bombings targeting post office, market areas and hospital in Maiduguri At least 23 people have been killed and more than 100 others injured in multiple suspected suicide bombings in the north-eastern Nigerian city of Maiduguri, shattering its reputation as a relative oasis of calm in recent years as a long-running insurgency was pushed to the rural hinterlands. Authorities said the explosions went off at the post office and market areas, as well as the entrance to the University of Maiduguri teaching hospital, on Monday evening during iftar, the breaking of fast in the month of Ramadan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 23 people killed in suspected suicide attacks in north-eastern Nigeria

events.title: 23 killed in Nigeria suicide attack
events.description: First, fresh violence is sweeping across Nigeria. Fifteen people killed in Katsina State while deadly explosions rock the northeastern city of Maiduguri, leaving at least 23 dead. Also, in the DR Congo, talk of a third term for President Félix Tshisekedi is growing louder. As allies openly back the idea, tensions are rising with an opposition that says the constitution must be respected. And in South Africa, tens of thousands of abused children never get the chance to speak in court: too traumatised to testify. Now, an unusual ally is stepping in: specially trained dogs helping young victims find the courage to tell their story.

events.title: Trump’s threats to ‘take’ Cuba signal rising US pressure as island grapples with power crisis
events.description: National power outage is making life extremely difficult and may force Havana into biggest economic changes in 67 years Just a few hours after a nationwide electricity blackout struck Cuba, Donald Trump hinted at an even darker future for the island’s rulers. The country’s entire electricity system had collapsed on Monday afternoon, leaving about 10 million people without power. Emergency teams were still struggling to restore it when the US leader made his latest threat. Continue reading...

events.title: War in Iran exposes cracks in Trump's MAGA coalition
events.description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. Kent, a former Green Beret and political candidate with connections to right-wing extremists, was in charge of an agency tasked with analyzing and detecting terrorist threats. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in Iran exposes cracks in Trump's MAGA coalition

events.title: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
events.description: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert

events.title: War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief
events.description: The disruption of global energy supplies is being felt worldwide, the UN’s top climate change official warned on Monday, as conflict in the Middle East drives oil and gas prices sharply higher – echoing the market turmoil triggered by the war in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief

events.title: Live: Israel strikes central Beirut as Iran attack leaves two dead near Tel Aviv
events.description: An Israeli strike on central Beirut – which came without warning – has left at least six dead and 24 injured. Meanwhile shrapnel from an Iranian missile launch killed two people in a Tel Aviv suburb, hours after Iran confirmed its security chief had been killed in an air strike. Follow our live blog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Live: Israel strikes central Beirut as Iran attack leaves two dead near Tel Aviv

events.title: Top Iranian officials killed in Israeli airstrike in wartime blow to country's leadership
events.description: Iran has confirmed the deaths of two senior security officials, considered among the most powerful figures in the country, in overnight strikes: Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, and General Gholamreza Soleimani, head of the Revolutionary Guard’s Basij force. It's a major blow to Iran’s complicated security apparatus. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell takes a look at how it works
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Top Iranian officials killed in Israeli airstrike in wartime blow to country's leadership

events.title: A timeline of attacks in Nigeria's northeastern Borno state
events.description: At least 23 people were killed and more than 100 wounded in suspected suicide bombings Monday night that targeted Maiduguri city in northeastern Nigeria, police said Tuesday
events.ticker_label: 💥 A timeline of attacks in Nigeria's northeastern Borno state

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counterstrikes reported across the region as the humanitarian toll rises. UN agencies warn that health systems are under growing strain, children are increasingly affected by violence and displacement, and hundreds of thousands have been forced from their homes - especially in Lebanon. Despite mounting logistical and security challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region

events.description: Hamas reportedly reasserts control in Gaza as Iran war shifts international focus, according to analysts raising serious doubts about postwar planning.

events.description: Donald Trump's most senior counterterrorism official has resigned, saying he "cannot in good conscience" support the Iran war and claiming the country posed "no imminent threat" to the United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about Joe Kent - the top US counterterrorism official who has resigned over Trump's Iran war

events.title: In Beirut, Guterres demands end to Middle East war: Civilians ‘deserve to live without fear’
events.description: The UN Secretary-General on Saturday called on the international community to intensify support for the Government and people of Lebanon, warning that the south of the country “risks being turned into a wasteland.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In Beirut, Guterres demands end to Middle East war: Civilians ‘deserve to live without fear’

events.title: Trump seeks to delay China summit as Vance denies ‘wedge’ over Iran war
events.description: Pair attempt to strike united front amid reports vice-president skeptical over US-Israeli attack on Iran Donald Trump revealed that he had asked China to delay his forthcoming visit to Beijing while the war with Iran was continuing, as he attempted to strike a united front on Monday with his vice-president, JD Vance, who is believed to have been skeptical over attacking Tehran’s regime. Appearing together with Vance for the first time in two weeks, Trump said he did not think the conflict – which started on 28 February after the US and Israel opened hostilities – would be over this week but predicted victory would be achieved soon. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump seeks to delay China summit as Vance denies ‘wedge’ over Iran war

events.title: Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul
events.description: Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan’s overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul, according to Taliban authorities, and injured more than 250 others.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul

events.title: Isolated and exposed: can New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery withstand the global oil shock?
events.description: New Zealand economic growth tipped to overtake Australia’s this year but Middle East conflict casts a shadow over outlook Just as New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery shows flickers of improvement – with economists predicting its annual growth could surpass that of its larger neighbour Australia – it is facing a new threat: the war in the Middle East. New Zealand is particularly exposed to the energy shocks produced by the conflict – and to economic crises generally – with the small, isolated nation highly dependent on global trade and tourism. It is susceptible to disruptions in supply chains and shipping. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Isolated and exposed: can New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery withstand the global oil shock?

events.title: Middle East crisis: UN health agency releases emergency funds for Lebanon, Iraq, Syria
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) is releasing another $2 million in emergency funds to support health systems in Lebanon, Iraq and Syria as strikes against Iran by the US and Israeli continue amid counterstrikes across the Gulf and wider region by Tehran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East crisis: UN health agency releases emergency funds for Lebanon, Iraq, Syria

events.title: Human rights advocates: War with Iran could spark new age of nuclear proliferation
events.description: Could the war with Iran lead to a world with more nuclear weapons, not less? Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Zeid Raad Al Hussein, about a potential new age of nuclear proliferation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Human rights advocates: War with Iran could spark new age of nuclear proliferation

events.title: Middle East war’s ‘spiral of conflict’ drives mounting civilian toll
events.description: The widening war in the Middle East and its growing impact on civilians came under scrutiny at the UN in Geneva on Monday, as independent experts briefing the Human Rights Council warned of escalating violence following the onset of Israeli and US strikes on Iran and counterstrikes by Tehran and allied groups.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war’s ‘spiral of conflict’ drives mounting civilian toll

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 March: UN scales up aid as crisis deepens into third week
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East has entered its third week, with fighting continuing across the region and humanitarian needs rising. Oil prices continue to hover above $100 a barrel, while shipping disruptions and temporary flight suspensions are affecting travel and supply chains. Despite mounting challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid, warning that displacement, food insecurity and pressure on health systems are growing as the war's impact spreads. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 March: UN scales up aid as crisis deepens into third week

events.title: Cuba’s electrical grid collapses amid US oil blockade
events.description: Ten million people left without power in latest of outages that sparked violent protest last weekend Cuba’s national electric grid has collapsed, the country’s grid operator has said, leaving approximately 10 million people without power amid a US-imposed oil blockade that has crippled the island’s already obsolete generation system. The grid operator, UNE, said on social media on Monday that it was investigating the causes of the blackout, the latest in a series of widespread outages that last for hours or days and that last weekend sparked a rare violent protest in the communist-run country. Continue reading...

events.title: A Trump official quits over the Iran war and Israel kills 2 top Iranian commanders
events.description: U.S. counterterrorism official Joe Kent became the first senior Trump administration member to resign over the Iran war. Israel killed two top Iranian officials, dealing another blow to the regime.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A Trump official quits over the Iran war and Israel kills 2 top Iranian commanders

events.title: Iran war: NATO making 'mistake' on Hormuz, Trump says

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: NATO making 'mistake' on Hormuz, Trump says

events.title: World Cup 2026: Is the US-Iran war an ethical tipping point?
events.description: The US is the main host of the 2026 World Cup. But it's currently at war with Iran, which has qualified for the tournament. The ethical conflicts of this soccer competition grow more complex each week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Cup 2026: Is the US-Iran war an ethical tipping point?

events.title: Cuba plunged into islandwide blackout as power crisis worsens, in photos
events.description: Officials in Cuba reported an islandwide blackout Monday as deepening energy and economic crises continue to strain a crumbling power grid. It marked the third major outage in four months. A separate blackout just over a week ago affected western Cuba, leaving millions without electricity. President Miguel Díaz-Canel warned Friday that the island has gone more than three months without oil shipments, relying instead on solar power, natural gas and aging thermoelectric plants. Cuba has blamed the crisis on a U.S. energy blockade, while U.S. President Donald Trump warned in January of tariffs on any country that sells or provides oil to the island. This is a photo gallery curated by AP photo editors.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cuba plunged into islandwide blackout as power crisis worsens, in photos

events.title: Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger
events.description: The Middle East war could cause the worst disruption to lifesaving humanitarian work since COVID, the UN World Food Programme (WFP) warned on Tuesday, as the UN chief again demanded an end to the widening conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger

events.title: Why Gulf states aren’t joining the war against Iran — despite attacks on their soil
events.description: Gulf states maintain restraint despite reported Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar and Kuwait, prioritizing regional stability.

events.title: ‘Hope’ for Haiti’s political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections
events.description: Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers “a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people”, according to the UN’s most senior official in the Caribbean country.

events.title: Starmer tells Zelensky focus must remain on Ukraine despite war in Iran
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky that international attention must remain on Ukraine's fight against Russia despite the US-Israeli war with Iran. The two leaders met in London and signed a new defence partnership focused on countering drones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Starmer tells Zelensky focus must remain on Ukraine despite war in Iran

events.title: War on Iran: As top leaders and commanders are killed, 'regime will harden and close ranks'
events.description: As Israel announces it has killed Iran's security chief, Ali Larijani, Annette Young is joined by Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London. In assessing the reported killing of senior Iranian leadership figures, Ms. Angha argues that such developments represent a serious shock to the Iranian political system but not an existential threat to the regime itself. The Islamic Republic was deliberately designed with layered succession structures. What we are likely to see is the further hardening of the regime’s security apparatus.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War on Iran: As top leaders and commanders are killed, 'regime will harden and close ranks'

events.title: Iranian strikes hit near Australian airbase in UAE, Albanese confirms

The prime minister, Anthony Albanese, confirmed that no Australian personnel were injured in the strike at 9.15am AEDT on Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

events.title: Indian tankers carrying LPG arrive at port after safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Two Indian-flagged tankers together carrying over 92,000 metric tonnes of liquefied petroleum gas have arrived at ports in Gujarat state after safely passing through the Strait of Hormuz. New Delhi has been negotiating with Iran, but the government denies discussing the possible release of Iranian tankers it seized in February as part of the bargain. Meanwhile, global crude prices jumped again amid new Iranian attacks on the UAE.

events.title: Colombian president accuses Ecuador after ‘27 charred bodies’ found near border
events.description: Relations deteriorate as Gustavo Petro claims government of Trump ally Daniel Noboa bombing targets in Colombia President Gustavo Petro has accused Ecuador of bombing targets inside Colombian territory, saying later that the burned remains of nearly 30 people had been found near the border, in a sharp deterioration in relations between the two neighbouring countries. The Colombian leader said on Tuesday that an attack which had left “27 charred bodies” did not appear to have been carried out by Colombia’s own forces or any illegal armed groups which he said do not have armed planes. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Colombian president accuses Ecuador after ‘27 charred bodies’ found near border

events.title: Cory Booker calls both parties ‘feckless’ for ceding war powers to Trump
events.description: Democrat says Congress ‘doing nothing’ may embolden president to attack countries such as Cuba and North Korea Democratic US senator Cory Booker has criticized both his own political party as well as its Republican counterpart for being “feckless” in ceding congressional war powers to Donald Trump , saying that their decision could embolden the president to unilaterally attack Cuba, North Korea and other countries. “I’m going to be one of those Democrats [who] say I think both parties have been feckless in allowing the growth of the power of the presidency,” Booker said on Sunday on CNN’s State of the Union. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cory Booker calls both parties ‘feckless’ for ceding war powers to Trump

events.title: Apple cuts China App Store commission fees after government pressure
events.description: The move, which lowers fees to 25%, is a breakthrough for Chinese developers Tencent and ByteDance Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Apple announced late on Thursday it would lower the commission fees collected in its App Store in mainland China . The move follows pressure from regulators in the tech company’s second-largest market, as well as global scrutiny of its payment requirements. Fees for in-app purchases and paid transactions will be lowered to 25% from 30% starting on Sunday, Apple said in a statement on its blog for developers. Continue reading...

events.title: As South Australia’s Liberals struggle to stay in the fight, will their preferences usher in One Nation?
events.description: Putting Pauline Hanson’s party above Labor on how-to-vote cards is a gamble for Ashton Hurn’s leadership, with the Liberals facing potential eclipse Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast South Australian politics rarely takes a central position in national news, but this Saturday’s state election will be watched much more closely than usual for potential federal implications. It is shaping up as a perfect storm for a struggling Liberal party: under pressure from a popular first-term Labor government, while One Nation eats into the Liberal base. The election will be a first test of One Nation’s growing support nationally, with federal polls showing Pauline Hanson’s party regularly outpolling a weakened Coalition. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ As South Australia’s Liberals struggle to stay in the fight, will their preferences usher in One Nation?

events.title: Nigeria tightens security in Maiduguri after suicide attacks

events.ticker_label: 💥 Nigeria tightens security in Maiduguri after suicide attacks

events.title: Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war
events.description: US president had earlier hinted trip could be put on hold if President Xi does not help unblock the strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis – live updates Trump has asked to delay his planned visit to Beijing by about a month due to the Iran war, after earlier hinting he might put the trip off if his prospective hosts do not help to unblock the strait of Hormuz. The US president’s summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meant to take place at the end of March but Trump told reporters in the White House on Monday: “Because of the war I want to be here, I have to be here, I feel. And so we’ve requested that we delay it a month or so.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war

events.title: Australia news live: high court orders removal of ankle bracelets and end of curfews for 43 noncitizens released from detention centres
events.description: Follow today’s news live Iranian strikes hit near Australian airbase in UAE, Albanese confirms Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast ARN confirms Kyle Sandilands contract terminated ARN just issued a statement confirming Kyle Sandilands’ contract had been terminated and the Kyle and Jackie O show cancelled. ARN has just announced that they’ve terminated my contract. I don’t accept it. My lawyers told them last week this would be invalid. And guess what? It is. They sacked Jackie. They suspended me. They wouldn’t even let me pick up the phone to call her or anyone else on the show. Then – and this is the bit that gets me – once they’d made it impossible for the show to go on, they turn around and say, “You didn’t fix it. You’re fired!” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia news live: high court orders removal of ankle bracelets and end of curfews for 43 noncitizens released from detention centres

events.title: Rubio calls for new Cuban leaders as latest blackout underscores deepening crisis

events.title: Africa particularly vulnerable as Iran conflict disrupts supply chains, say experts
events.description: Food production in many African countries depends heavily on fertiliser imported from the Gulf through the strait of Hormuz Countries in Africa, where farmers depend heavily on imported fertiliser and a large share of household income goes on food, are particularly vulnerable to supply chain disruptions caused by the war in the Middle East, experts have said. The conflict has drastically disrupted trade through the strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane not just for oil and gas but also for fertiliser, which is produced in vast quantities in the Gulf. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Africa particularly vulnerable as Iran conflict disrupts supply chains, say experts

events.title: War in Iran: Chronically ill fight for survival
events.description: As a consequence of the US-Israeli war against Iran, a new humanitarian crisis is looming. Millions of chronically ill people in Iran are at risk of losing access to medical care.

events.title: Russian tanker adrift in Mediterranean poses 'imminent and serious' threat, EU states say

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: ‘These connections are overlooked’: how British companies profited from slavery in Brazil long after abolition
events.description: Britons learn about the country’s involvement ‘almost as a self-congratulatory narrative’, says historian Joseph Mulhern In 1845 British citizens and companies were already legally prohibited from owning or buying enslaved people overseas, yet that year 385 captives were “transferred” to a British mining company in Brazil named St John d’El Rey. Despite a global campaign waged by the UK against slavery and the transatlantic slave trade, the move was not technically illegal because the enslaved people were not sold but “rented” – a practice permitted overseas under the 1843 Slave Trade Act. Continue reading...

events.title: Man and woman charged with murder of Iranian activist in Canada
events.description: Charges follow discovery of body of Masood Masjoody, who was a critic of the Tehran regime and the exiled shah Two people have been charged with the murder of an Iranian activist in Canada, in a case which has intensified fears over transnational repression of critics of the regime in Tehran. Masood Masjoody, a former university maths teacher, went missing in early February in the city of Burnaby, British Columbia. He had been critical of Iran’s theocratic regime and the exiled family of the former shah. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man and woman charged with murder of Iranian activist in Canada

events.title: Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader
events.description: Family of then PM, Patrice Lumumba, welcome decision to charge Étienne Davignon as ‘beginning of a reckoning’ A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled. Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes. The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga. The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men. Depriving them of a fair trial. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader

events.title: Nearly 5 million children are still dying annually before their fifth birthday: Here’s why

events.title: Deepfakes, voice cloning and weaponised AI: Global wake-up call to organised fraud

As their retirement age approached, the idea of a low-risk investment for their pension seemed attractive. But one day, after clicking on a seemingly legitimate online advert that offered a reasonable risk-averse plan, they unlocked a process that would lead them to lose over $2.5 million.

events.title: 'Shocking' ruling strips Senegal of AFCON title, declares Morocco winner
events.description: Morocco was stunningly awarded the Africa Cup of Nations title on Tuesday by appeals judges who overturned Senegal’s victory in a chaotic final in January. The Confederation of African Football said its appeals board ruled that Senegal is “declared to have forfeited the final” and its 1-0 win in extra time becomes a 3-0 default win for host nation Morocco. FRANCE 24's James Vasina and sports journalist Karim Baldé discuss the ruling.

events.title: Larijani was 'key to Iran's reach in the Gulf region'
events.description: Reporting from Doha for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iranian security chief Ali Larijani "was key to Iran's reach to the Gulf region" adding that "the GCC and Iran were two parties deeply suspicious of each other, it took decades for them to get close to each other and Larijani played a big role in that". Ali Larijani was reported to have died in an Israeli airstrike on Tuesday.

events.title: Myki-less public transport for all passengers won’t happen until 2027, Victorian auditor reveals
events.description: Dispute between government and US-based contractor caused 18-month delay to project, report finds Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Myki cards will still be needed by Victorian public transport users until 2027 due to delays to the full rollout of tap-and-go technology, the state’s auditor general has revealed. A report by the Victorian Auditor-General’s Office (Vago) found a dispute between the state’s transport department and US-based firm Conduent had delayed the project by 18 months, adding $136.8m to its cost. Continue reading...

events.title: How you walk reveals to others how you are feeling, researchers say
events.description: Study highlights the movements in people’s gait that give away most about their emotional state A long face is not the only sign that someone is down in the dumps. How people walk is revealing too, particularly the swing of the arms and legs, researchers say. Scientists asked volunteers to guess people’s emotions from video clips of them walking and found that bigger swings portrayed more aggression while smaller swings implied fear and sadness. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 How you walk reveals to others how you are feeling, researchers say

events.title: Iran arrests dozens accused of spying for Israel in new internal crackdown

events.title: Spanish king reopens debate on conquest of Mexico by acknowledging 'abuse'

events.title: Aspiring female Zambian politicians were asked for sexual favours, official says

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Chinese fishing 'militia' formations signal rising gray-zone pressure on Taiwan
events.description: China's maritime militia allegedly deployed over 2,000 fishing boats in coordinated formations near Taiwan, raising significant security concerns among analysts.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Cuba's entire electrical grid collapses, leaving whole island without power
events.description: Cuba's power grid suffers total collapse amid fuel shortages and sanctions, leaving the island nation scrambling to restore electricity to millions.

events.title: Chile's president begins building border barrier less than week into term
events.description: José Antonio Kast visited the ditch - a few feet wide and deep - on Monday, calling it a "milestone".

events.description: It comes weeks after a Republican lawmaker accused the justice department of a "cover-up" in releasing the Epstein files.

events.description: US defence secretary designated Anthropic a 'supply chain risk' after it refused to remove guardrails on its technology.

events.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi says US-Israeli assassinations will not topple Tehran's 'strong political structure'.

events.title: Ukraine sends 201 military experts to counter Iranian drones in the Gulf
events.description: Ukraine President Zelenskyy says Moscow, Tehran 'brothers in hatred'; claims Iran's drones 'contain Russian components'.

events.title: Xi Jinping’s anticorruption drive sweeps up senior Chinese military chiefs
events.description: Experts say rooting out corruption in the military remains a priority for China's President Xi.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Nigeria’s online content creator market has boomed. Can the skit-makers and streamers make it pay?
events.description: As platforms make less from advertising, creators are struggling to monetise work – leading to calls for more government investment and tax breaks On a humid afternoon in Lagos, a shoot for a comedy skit is under way on a set that looks more like a small film production. Dozens of people mill about: lighting assistants, a sound engineer, a makeup artist and even a content creator recording unscripted behind-the-scenes footage. At the centre is Broda Shaggi, born Samuel Animashaun Perry, who is issuing instructions, rehearsing lines and performing caricatures. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump lashes out at NATO after countries decline to help in Iran
events.description: President Trump says NATO is making "a very foolish mistake" after countries decline to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah
events.description: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah

events.title: Who was Ali Larijani, Iran's unofficial strongman?
events.description: Veteran politician Ali Larijani came from a powerful family — but was never fully embraced by Iranian hardliners.

events.title: AFCON: Senegal stripped of title, Morocco crowned champions
events.description: Morocco have been crowned Africa Cup of Nations champions after a major ruling stripped Senegal of the title. Senegal intends to appeal the unprecedented decision.

events.title: Vatican declares partial mistrial in cardinal's fraud case
events.description: A Vatican appeals court overturned the conviction in a landmark fraud case involving a senior Catholic cardinal. Angelo Becciu is appealing a five-and-a-half year jail term.

events.title: Paris Saint-Germain crush Chelsea to reach Champions League quarter-finals
events.description: The French team swept into the quarter-finals of the UEFA Champions League on Tuesday with a 3-0 win at Chelsea FC, sealing an emphatic 8-2 aggregate victory in their last-16 tie.

events.title: Senegal will appeal decision that gives AFCON title to Morocco
events.description: The Senegalese football team says Tuesday they will appeal after Confederation of African Football controversially stripped them of their Africa Cup of Nations title over a walk-off during the final in January and instead declared Morocco continental champions.

events.title: Congo-Brazzaville’s Sassou Nguesso reaps landslide re-election win, provisional results suggest
events.description: Congo-Brazzaville’s incumbent President Denis Sassou Nguesso has been re-elected for a fifth term in a landslide win, provisional results showed Tuesday. Sassou Nguesso, 82, is one of Africa’s longest-serving leaders, and has been at the helm for a total of 42 years. The main opposition boycotted the vote, describing it as a farce.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Congo-Brazzaville’s Sassou Nguesso reaps landslide re-election win, provisional results suggest

events.title: France’s ex-president Sarkozy maintains innocence in Libya funding appeal trial
events.description: France’s former president Nicolas Sarkozy on Tuesday maintained his innocence as he returned to court to appeal his conviction for seeking financing from Libya’s late dictator Muammar Gaddafi to fund his 2007 presidential campaign. If Sarkozy loses his appeal, he faces up to 10 years in prison.

events.title: Cubans abroad will be able to invest, own businesses on island
events.description: Cubans living abroad will now be allowed to invest and own businesses on the island, a senior government official said Monday, unveiling a key reform as the economy struggles under intense US pressure.

events.title: Senegal stripped of Africa Cup of Nations over misconduct in final
events.description: Senegal has been stripped of the African Cup of Nations after misconduct in the tournament final, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) has ruled.

events.title: Princess of Wales celebrates St Patrick's Day with Irish Guards
events.description: The Princess of Wales wished the officers and guardsmen of the Irish Guards a happy St Patrick's Day as she presented them with traditional sprigs of shamrock.

events.title: Peru’s prime minister resigns ahead of congressional confirmation vote
events.description: Peru’s prime minister has resigned from her post

events.title: Republic of Congo’s President N’Guesso reelected to his fifth consecutive term, according to provisional results
events.description: Republic of Congo’s President N’Guesso reelected to his fifth consecutive term, according to provisional results

events.title: Rome's Colosseum gets a fresh look that recreates the footprints of long-gone columns
events.description: The Colosseum has a bright new look following a restoration using the same travertine marble of ancient Rome to recreate parts of columns from 2,000 years ago

events.title: Shigeaki Mori, Hiroshima atomic bomb survivor and historian, dies at 88
events.description: Japanese atomic bomb survivor Shigeaki Mori has died at age 88
events.ticker_label: 💥 Shigeaki Mori, Hiroshima atomic bomb survivor and historian, dies at 88

events.title: Vatican appeals court declares mistrial in 'trial of the century' against cardinal
events.description: The Vatican appeals tribunal has declared a mistrial in the Holy See’s big “trial of the century.”

events.title: France’s far right makes gains in first-round municipal elections
events.description: France’s far right has made gains in the first round of municipal elections in the southeast, while big cities head to a runoff, including Paris, Marseille, and Lyon

events.title: Budapest's vintage freight trams celebrate 100 years in service
events.description: Budapest is keeping century-old freight trams running to do the work that modern passenger trams cannot, plowing snow and hauling broken-down vehicles

events.title: Migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean. Authorities are withholding information
events.description: The beginning of 2026 ranks as the deadliest start to any year for people trying to cross the Mediterranean

events.title: Guterres urges action against ‘rising tide of anti-Muslim hatred’
events.description: The UN chief on Monday urged countries to “work together” and eradicate a rising tide of anti-Muslim hate, calling for a rejection of “the narratives of fear and exclusion”.

events.title: Florida executes convict in 2008 rape, killing of woman, whose final call sparked 911 reforms
events.description: Florida executed Michael Lee King for the 2008 murder of Denise Amber Lee, who called 911 begging for help while tied up in his car during the crime.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Florida executes convict in 2008 rape, killing of woman, whose final call sparked 911 reforms

events.title: 23 arrested in alleged panga illegal immigrant smuggling plot off California coast
events.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection intercepted a panga-style boat near San Clemente Island on Saturday, and 23 people were detained and turned over to ICE.

events.title: Drug kingpin on parole caught with enough fentanyl to kill most of county, DA says
events.description: Convicted drug trafficker gets 159 years for pumping enough fentanyl into Colorado to potentially kill 88% of Weld County's population, prosecutors say.

events.title: Multiple allies decline US calls for Strait of Hormuz support amid rising Middle East tensions
events.description: Several U.S. allies are refusing military involvement in securing the Strait of Hormuz, citing diplomacy, legal limits or strategic caution.

events.title: American flag raised, flies over US Embassy building in Venezuela for first time in 7 years
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Caracas raised the American flag for the first time in seven years, months after former President Nicolás Maduro's capture by U.S. forces.
